accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context; the rule did establish tax reporting requirements for brokers in digital asset sales.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the CRA name, IRS as issuer, and description of the rule are accurate context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the resolution disapproves and nullifies the IRS digital asset broker reporting rule.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly explains the rule is nullified under the CRA and will have no force or effect.

exceptions_conditions.why: A one-line CRA disapproval resolution contains no material exceptions or carve-outs.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states House passage, Senate passage, and approval dates matching the bill's legislative history.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative effect: nullification of the identified IRS rule.

objective_tone.why: Summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

concise.why: Brief and free of padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the IRS, the rule title, the Congressional Review Act, and chapter 8 of title 5.

correct_figures.why: Federal Register citation (89 Fed. Reg. 106928), publication date, and all legislative dates and the public law number are correct.
